zh.Manor Marham, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可馬哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord於大約1271年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可時，他的父親William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,43歲，母親Margaret de Marham33歲。他和Avice Bardolf至少有4個兒子。他去世於1313年4月11日在他的家鄉享年43歲。

zh-hans.Manor Marham, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可馬哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord于大约1271年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可时，他的父亲William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,43岁，母亲Margaret de Marham33岁。他和Avice Bardolf至少有4个儿子。他去世于1313年4月11日在他的家乡享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Belet,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfonso, Eduardo, Jere, Jorge, Jose, Luis, Migdalia.
Catalan: from the Catalan form of the Latin personal name Benedictus (see Benedict ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic and Sephardic): from the personal name Benet, ultimately derived from the Latin Benedictus (compare 1 above) and used by Jews as a vernacular equivalent of Hebrew Baruch .

variants.name: Benedict
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: from the personal name Benedict, from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. This owed its popularity in the Middle Ages chiefly to Saint Benedict of Norcia (c. 480–550), who founded the Benedictine order of monks at Monte Cassino and wrote a monastic rule that formed a model for all subsequent rules. No doubt the meaning of the Latin word also contributed to its popularity as a personal name, especially in Romance countries. Occasionally the English surname may derive from Latin benedicite ‘bless (you)’, perhaps given as a nickname to an habitual user of the expression. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Benedikt and many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Benedek , Slovenian Benedik (see Benedick ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Italian Benedetti .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Knight,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Inghram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is derived from a medieval given name. This was originally a contracted form of the Norman name Engelram, composed of the ethnic name Engel ‘Angle’ (or Ing, name of the Old Norse fertility god) + hramn ‘raven’. As late as the 17th century Engelram and Ingram were still in occasional use as given names in the north of England.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord was born about 1271, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,, was 43 and his mother, Margaret de Marham, was 33. He had at least 4 sons with Avice Bardolf. He died on 11 April 1313, in his hometown, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord est né environ 1271, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Marham, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Avice Bardolf. Il est décédé le 11 avril 1313, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord um 1271 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight, 43 und seine Mutter Margaret de Marham 33. Er hatte mit Avice Bardolf mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 11. April 1313 im Alter von 43 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord è nato il circa 1271 nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Marham, aveva 33 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli con Avice Bardolf. Morì il 11 aprile 1313, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 43 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord(이)가 대략 1271년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight,(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Marham(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Avice Bardolf(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1313년 4월 11일 그의 고향에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Inghram De Belet, of Thorp.Belet, Knight, Lord родился около 1271 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Belet Alias Bigot of Marham, Sir Knight, было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Marham, 33. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Avice Bardolf. Он умер 11 апреля 1313 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 43 лет.
